title: Sub-Actions (Self-Referencing Hierarchy) — NXGN Actions
purpose: **Scope**: This document describes the patterns for self-referencing entity hierarchies. Actions uses `ParentActionId` on `ActionEntity` to support sub-actions (child actions under a parent). This is a simplified version of Capstone's full TreeNode pattern — no TPH, no path materialization, no discriminators.

sections.title: Overview
content: public class ActionEntity : TrackedEntity, ITenantEntity, ISoftDeletable, IVersioned
- Sub-actions nested under a parent action
- Guard conditions that check "all sub-actions are in terminal state"
- Hierarchical action screens (parent with child list)

sections.title: EF Configuration
content: - `OnDelete(DeleteBehavior.Restrict)` — deleting a parent is prevented while children exist. Children must be reassigned or deleted first (matches the [EF Configuration](ef-configuration.md) rule: `Restrict` for cross-aggregate references).
- `WithMany()` without a navigation collection — we don't load all children via the parent nav prop (use queries instead).
- Composite index on `(TenantId, ParentActionId)` for efficient child lookups under RLS.

sections.title: DTOMapper: Parent Reference
content: The parent is mapped as a `NamedDTO` (ID + display name), not a full nested DTO:
// The ParentAction field is part of ActionDTO alongside the other fields
// Inherits NamedDTO — Name/Description come from translations
public string ReferenceNumber { get; set; } = string.Empty;
**GridItemDTO** flattens the parent to a scalar:
public string ReferenceNumber { get; set; } = string.Empty;

sections.title: EntityMapper: Setting Parent
content: The EntityMapper reads the `ParentAction.Id` from the DTO and sets the FK:
**Key rule:** The EntityMapper sets the FK scalar (`ParentActionId`), never the
navigation property. EF resolves the relationship from the FK.

sections.title: Guard Condition: Sub-Action Dependencies
content: The workflow engine evaluates a guard condition that checks all sub-actions are
in terminal state before allowing a parent action to transition:
ActionEntity action, ActionsDbContext db, CancellationToken ct)
$"{openChildren} sub-action(s) are not in a terminal state");
This guard is evaluated as part of the deterministic 9-guard chain documented in

sections.title: Descendant Queries
content: For operations that need all descendants (e.g., cascade soft-delete, impact
await using var db = await dbFactory.CreateDbContextAsync(ct);
- Load only the columns needed (`Id`, `ParentActionId`) — not full entities
- Stack-based traversal — never recursion (avoids stack overflow on deep hierarchies)
- Exclude the root from results (descendants only, not self)

sections.title: Frontend: Embedded Sub-Action List
content: Sub-actions are displayed as an embedded list on the action capture page:
The sub-action embedded list follows the standard [Embedded List](../web/embedded-list.md)
pattern. Sub-actions are full actions with their own capture page — the embedded list

sections.title: Differences from Capstone TreeNode
content: | Inheritance | TPH with discriminator (OrgNode, DisciplineNode) | Single entity (ActionEntity) |
| Path | Materialized path per-translation (`"Root->Parent->Child"`) | No path — parent reference only |
| Path updates | Three-pass mapper (properties → parents → paths) | Single-pass (just set ParentActionId) |
| Depth | Arbitrary depth (org hierarchies) | Typically 1-2 levels (parent → sub-actions) |
| TreeNodeMap | Dual-index lookup (by ID and path) | Standard EntityMap (by ID only) |
| Tree service | TreeNodeService for path building, descendant traversal | Inline descendant query |
| Attribute values | Per-node custom attributes (TreeNodeAttributeValue) | None — actions use standard fields |
The simpler pattern is intentional — Actions' hierarchy is shallow (parent with
sub-actions), not a deep tree like Capstone's org structure.
